name: aws
description: AWS cloud power pack — IAM least-privilege, S3 exposure, Terraform/CDK correctness, network security, and cost guardrails
rules: IAM policies must be least-privilege — no Action:* with Resource:*
S3 buckets never public by default; enable Block Public Access at account + bucket
All S3 buckets must have SSE-KMS or SSE-S3 encryption enabled
RDS/EBS volumes encrypted at rest; snapshots must inherit encryption
Security Groups must not allow 0.0.0.0/0 on SSH (22), RDP (3389), or DB ports
Lambda functions must have explicit timeout, memory, and log retention
CloudTrail enabled in all regions with log file validation
Use parameter store / Secrets Manager — never hardcode credentials
Terraform state must be remote (S3 + DynamoDB lock) with encryption
Use resource tags for cost allocation (Environment, Owner, CostCenter)

checks.description: IAM policy with Action: '*' AND Resource: '*' grants unbounded access
AdministratorAccess managed policy attached — use scoped policy
S3 bucket ACL set to public-read or public-read-write
S3 bucket without aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block — risk of accidental public exposure
Security Group ingress 0.0.0.0/0 on port 22 (SSH) — attack surface
Security Group ingress 0.0.0.0/0 on port 3389 (RDP) — attack surface
Security Group ingress 0.0.0.0/0 on DB ports (3306/5432/1433/27017/6379)
Hardcoded AWS secret access key (40-char base64-like)
Lambda function without explicit timeout — defaults to 3s, may truncate work
Lambda without aws_cloudwatch_log_group retention policy — logs kept forever (cost + compliance)
Verify CloudTrail is configured across all regions with log file validation
Terraform state stored locally — use S3 backend + DynamoDB lock
Security Group egress 0.0.0.0/0 to all protocols — restrict when possible
